briefs.title: Council to consider: Replacing Burnaby's 1965 Zoning Bylaw with 2026 Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider repealing the current 1965 zoning bylaw and replacing it with a new Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026. The proposed bylaw includes updated guidelines for development permits and changes to zoning districts. Development permit guidelines cover form and character, streamside protection, and tenant protection. Zoning affects what you can build or do on your property.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed bylaw and zone reassignment maps to understand potential impacts.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body: 7.1 BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT 1. Burnaby Zoning Bylaw - Proposed Repeal and Replacement 2026-04-28.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 2026-04-28_.pdf 3. Attachment 2 - Proposed Form and Character Development Permit Area Guidelines 2026-04-28.pdf 4. Attachment 3 - Proposed Streamside Protection and Enhancement Development Permit Area Guidelines 2026-04-28.pdf 5. Attachment 4 - Proposed Tenant Protection Development Permit Area Guidelines 2026-04-28.pdf 6. Attachment 5 - Proposed Zone Reassignment Reference Maps 2026-04-28.pdf 7. Attachment 6 - Rental Replacement and Inclusionary Requirements Evaluation 2026-04-28.pdf 8. Attachment 7 - Example Inclusionary and Rental Replacement at Occupancy and Second Tenancy 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 , including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment. Burnaby Zoning Bylaw - Proposed Repeal and Replacement 2026-04-28.pdf Attachment 1 - Proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 2026-04-28_.pdf Attachment 2 - Proposed Form and Character Development Permit Area Guidelines 20 Attachment 3 - Proposed Streamside Protection and Enhancement Development Permit Attachment 4 - Proposed Tenant Protection Development Permit Area Guidelines 202 Attachment 5 - Proposed Zone Reassignment Reference Maps 2026-04-28.pdf Attachment 6 - Rental Replacement and Inclusionary Requirements Evaluation 2026- Attachment 7 - Example Inclusionary and Rental Replacement at Occupancy and Seco

briefs.title: Council to Vote on Funding for Burnaby Housing Authority
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving funding for the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA). The funding would support the BHA's operations for the coming year. Council is set to vote on the BHA's request for assistance with its Year 2 operating funds. If approved, this funding will help the BHA continue its work in the community.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Council decision to understand how it may affect housing initiatives.
briefs.source_body: 7.2 BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING 1. BHA 2026 Operating Funding 2026-04-28_.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - BHA Assistance Request for Year 2 Operating Funds 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding. BHA 2026 Operating Funding 2026-04-28_.pdf Attachment 1 - BHA Assistance Request for Year 2 Operating Funds 2026-04-28.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Vote on 2026 Burnaby Property Tax Rates for Businesses
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider the proposed property tax rates for 2026. These rates affect municipal, regional, and Business Improvement Area (BIA) taxes. Property taxes are a main way the City pays for services. Council will vote on the bylaw that sets these rates.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed rates to understand potential impacts on your business.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body: 7.3 2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES 1. 2026 Property Tax rates 2026-04-28__.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026_2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates. 2026 Property Tax rates 2026-04-28__.pdf Attachment 1 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026_2026-04-28.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Vote: Appointing an Approving Officer for City of Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider appointing an Approving Officer. This person is responsible for approving subdivision plans and other land-related applications. Council is set to vote on this appointment at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body: 7.4 APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER 1. Appointment of Approving Officer 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer. Appointment of Approving Officer 2026-04-28.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Variance Permit for Retaining Walls on 11th Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a request for a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property on 11th Avenue. The permit would allow changes to the height of retaining walls. A DVP lets a property owner vary or alter some of the requirements of City bylaws related to development. Council will vote on whether to approve DVP #26-01.
briefs.action_required: Review the permit details to see how this decision could affect your property.
briefs.source_body: 7.5 DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES 1. DVP26-01 - 8012 11th Avenue 2026-04-28.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - DVP 26-01 - Sketch1 and 2 2026-04-28.pdf 3. briefs.title: Council to Consider: Renewing City of Burnaby Insurance Contracts
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider renewing contracts for the City's property and liability insurance programs for 2026. These programs cover potential costs to the City from damage or lawsuits. Council will vote on awarding the contracts at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action required. This information is for your awareness.
briefs.source_body: 7.6 2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS 1. 2026 Insurance Program - Contract Renewals - 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs. 2026 Insurance Program - Contract Renewals - 2026-04-28.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Networking Software Licenses Contract Award
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving a contract for networking software licenses. This contract would cover the supply and delivery of these licenses. Council's vote will determine whether the City moves forward with this agreement.
briefs.action_required: No action required.
briefs.source_body: 7.8 CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES 1. CA - Networking Software Licenses 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. CA - Networking Software Licenses 2026-04-28.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Vote: New Civic Facility Furnishings and Equipment
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider awarding a contract for supplying, delivering, and installing furniture for the new civic facility. This contract covers office and other facility furniture. Council is set to vote on this at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action required.
briefs.source_body: 7.9 CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT 1. briefs.title: Council to Consider Contract Change for External Audit Services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving a change to the contract for external audit services. The purpose is to get Council's approval for this contract change. An external audit is when an outside company checks a business's or organization's financial records.
briefs.action_required: No action required.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex Geotechnical Consulting
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving a contract change for geotechnical (soil and rock) consulting services. These services relate to the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project. Council will vote on whether to approve this change at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action required.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Spring 2026 Community Grant Applications
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving the list of groups recommended to receive community grants for Spring 2026. These grants support various community projects. The grants are divided into categories like sports, general operations, new initiatives, subsidized facility use, and food security.
briefs.action_required: Check the list of recommended grant recipients if you applied for a City of Burnaby community grant.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Fees and Charges Bylaw Amendments for Mid-2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider changes to the city's fees and charges bylaw. The proposed bylaw would adjust certain fees starting in June and September 2026. These fees cover various city services and permits. Council is set to vote on these changes at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed fee changes to see if they affect your business's operating costs.

key_dates.label: Amended Schedule F fees take effect — Burnaby
Amended Schedule F3 fees take effect — Burnaby

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Food Service, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw for New Developments Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a new Tenant Protection Bylaw. The bylaw would offer more protection to renters when new developments are built. It aims to balance tenant rights and development. Council will also consider related changes to the city's bylaw enforcement rules. The rules would help the city enforce the new tenant protections.
briefs.action_required: If you are a landlord or tenant, familiarize yourself with the proposed bylaw.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project Improvements
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider approving proposed improvements to the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. The project aims to improve cycling infrastructure in the southeast area of Burnaby and the Big Bend area. Council will review the project scope and proposed changes at the next meeting.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed changes to the Southeast Bikeway.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Protected Intersection Design Standards Amendment in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on changes to the Town Centre Design Standards. A proposed bylaw would add rules for 'protected intersection geometry'. This aims to make roads safer for people walking and cycling, and to improve mobility for all ages and abilities.
briefs.action_required: No action required.
briefs.source_body: 8.5 TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY 1. TC - Proposed Amendment to the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility. TC - Proposed Amendment to the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards 2026-04-28.p

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Brentwood Park Restrictive Covenants, Building Scheme Exemptions
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider exempting properties in Brentwood Park with Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schemes from certain Provincial requirements. Restrictive Covenants are agreements that limit how land can be used. A building scheme is a set of rules that control the design and development of properties in an area.
briefs.action_required: If you own property in Brentwood Park, follow this issue as it moves through Council.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body: 9.1 BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES 1. Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schemes 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements. Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schem

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to Consider Building Permit Activity Report for March 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review a report on building permit activity for March 2026. The report gives Council information about construction happening in Burnaby, based on the building permits issued during that month. This is for informational purposes.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. This is an informational report.
briefs.source_body: 9.2 SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026 1. SU - Building Permit Activity Report No. 03 2026-04-28.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Building Permit Activity Report No. 03 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period. SU - Building Permit Activity Report No. 03 2026-04-28.pdf Attachment 1 - Building Permit Activity Report No. 03 2026-04-28.pdf

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to Consider Solid Waste and Recycling Annual Report
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review the Solid Waste and Recycling (SWR) Annual Report for 2025. The report summarizes how well the city's waste and recycling collection system performed. Council will consider the report's findings and may use them to inform future decisions about waste management.
briefs.action_required: No action required.
briefs.source_body: 9.4 EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT 1. EC - Solid Waste and Recycling (SWR) 2025 Annual Report 2026-04-28.pdf 2. Attachment 1 - Solid Waste and Recycling 2025 Annual Report 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance. EC - Solid Waste and Recycling (SWR) 2025 Annual Report 2026-04-28.pdf Attachment 1 - Solid Waste and Recycling 2025 Annual Report 2026-04-28.pdf

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to Consider Food Sustainability Measures
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review an update on the city's food sustainability plans. The report covers current efforts to improve local food production and access. It also suggests ways to make the food system more environmentally friendly and resilient.
briefs.industries: Agriculture, Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail
briefs.source_body: 9.5 EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY 1. EC - SU Enhancing Food Sustainability in Burnaby 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby. EC - SU Enhancing Food Sustainability in Burnaby 2026-04-28.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Safety Improvements at Central Boulevard and Parkade Intersection
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal aims to improve safety for drivers and pedestrians in this area. Council will review an overview of the planned changes at the next meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action required.
briefs.source_body: 9.6 TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS 1. TC - Central Boulevard and Parkade Intersection Safety Improvements - 2026-04-28.pdf Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. TC - Central Boulevard and Parkade Intersection Safety Improvements - 2026-04-28

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Adding Hotel, Restaurant, and Housing Units to Grand Promenade
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider amending a rezoning bylaw for 3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place. The changes would allow the addition of hotel and restaurant uses, plus 362 more housing units. The applicant also wants to move an inclusionary zoning requirement to another site.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Council decision if you operate a business in the area.
briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body: 11.1.1 #14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place) 1. 14808 Amend No. 7, 2026 REZ 24-23 (Grand Promenade_Austin Rd_Gatineau PL).ACD 2026.pdf Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval. (Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026) 14808 Amend No. 7, 2026 REZ 24-23 (Grand Promenade_Austin Rd_Gatineau PL).ACD 20

briefs.title: Proposed: 12-Storey Mixed-Use Development at 6535 Kingsway Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a zoning change to allow a new 12-storey building at 6535 Kingsway. The building would have 290 apartments, commercial space on the ground floor, and underground parking. The proposal fits with the City's official community plan (OCP) — a document that guides future growth.
briefs.action_required: No action required.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body: 11.1.2 #14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway) 1. 14809 Amendment No. 8, 2026 REZ 25-09 (6535 Kingsway).CD 2026.pdf Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site. (Item 8.3., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026) 14809 Amendment No. 8, 2026 REZ 25-09 (6535 Kingsway).CD 2026.pdf

briefs.title: Proposed: Five-Storey Self-Storage Building Zoning Amendment in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a zoning change for 2757 Norland Avenue. The proposed bylaw amendment would allow for a five-storey self-storage building. This change follows the City's official community plan and transitional rules.
briefs.action_required: None.
briefs.source_body: 11.1.3 #14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue) 1. 14810 Amendment No. 9, 2026 REZ 25-21 (2757 Norland Ave).CD 2026.pdf Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions. (Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026) 14810 Amendment No. 9, 2026 REZ 25-21 (2757 Norland Ave).CD 2026.pdf

briefs.title: Council to Consider: E-Bike Pilot Program Bylaw Amendment in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider amending the Street and Traffic Bylaw to create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. The proposed bylaw would change the rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets. This is not yet approved.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the pilot program details as they develop.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: E-Bike Pilot Program in Burnaby Parks
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a change to the city's Parks Regulation Bylaw. The proposed bylaw would create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters in some parks. Council will vote on whether to approve the program.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed on the decision, as it may affect park access for your business or employees.

briefs.title: Proposed: E-bike, Scooter Program Changes to Burnaby Bylaw Enforcement
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider changes to the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. The proposed changes relate to the city's e-bike and electric kick scooter programs. The changes would affect how the city enforces rules about these programs. A bylaw is a local law.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the council's decision, as it may affect e-bike/scooter programs.

briefs.title: Council to Vote: Burnaby 2026 Property Tax Rates Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a bylaw to set property tax rates for 2026. This bylaw would decide how much tax owners of land and buildings in Burnaby will pay. The rules are based on sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter, which gives the city power to set these rates.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed tax rates and consider how they might affect your business.
briefs.source_body: 11.2.4 #14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 1. 14814.Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 v2.pdf Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter (Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council April 28, 2026) Subject to approval of item 7.3. 14814.Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 v2.pdf
